summary:
Alanine transfers ammonia from the brain through glucose-alanine cycle, facilitating its conversion to less toxic substances before excretion while also protecting neurons from toxicity. This process involves both energy production support and detoxification functions essential for maintaining neurological health. The primary mechanism relies heavily on alanine’s unique ability within these metabolic pathways. To summarize concisely focusing only on critical aspects related directly addressing your query about ammonia removal specifically linked back clinically,

Ammonia leaves the brain primarily using alanine-based transfer processes which convert it safely outside neural tissues preventing buildup leading potential damage thereby supporting overall cognitive function stability crucially dependent upon effective interorgan communication involving liver kidneys etc.. Key here being efficiency safety & systemic benefit provided mainly due alaniines specialized role during entire journey post-exit until complete neutralisation occurs naturally elsewhere body .
